meaning: adj.死后的,身后的,作者死后出版的,遗腹的
examples: The comic novel, The Posthumous Papers of the Pickwick Club, appeared serially in 1836 and 1837, and was first published in book form in 1837.
"Keiun" comes from the posthumous name of the former owner.
All but one have been posthumous.
The play coincides with a growing campaign to award Tull a posthumous Military Cross.
The posthumous award marked the bravery of a young child in the face of death.
